blocks.text: Hound is a Deno-native job queue. Sorted-set delivery, crash recovery via the Reaper, cron, and a management API — all in one package backed by a single Redis connection.
It solves background jobs in Deno without the BullMQ / Sidekiq baggage: at-least-once delivery, crash recovery, cron, type-safe emit, and an HTTP / WebSocket gateway, shipped as a single import.

items: InMemoryStorage for tests and single-process apps
delay, attempts, retryDelayMs, retryBackoff (fixed / exponential), priority, explicit id
Cron via repeat.pattern — self-rescheduling, survives restart
Exponential retry backoff capped at 1 hour, DLQ via processor.dlq.streamKey
Reaper sweeps stalled jobs every visibilityTimeoutMs/2 (min 5s)
Lua-script claim is atomic — N workers safely consume the same queue
Codegen: generateTypes + generateClient → typed HoundJobMap + portable HoundClient (Node / Bun / browser)

blocks.text: All Redis on bare metal. MacBook Pro 16" M1 Pro, no simulated work.

Ceiling is ~34k jobs/s on a single local Redis. Horizontal scale comes from running multiple Hound workers against one Redis — the Lua-script claim is atomic, so N workers safely consume the same queue.

Latency at max concurrency reflects queue depth, not per-job overhead — at 10c steady state, p99 sits at 14ms. Tune claimCount (default 200/poll) up if your jobs are short and Redis RTT is non-trivial.
Hound is open source under MIT, published to JSR, and used internally to power background work behind every Hushkey + Howl deployment.
